title: QUIC server-role ALPN selection on the EncryptedExtensions seam
purpose: Describe how the managed server-role handshake path preserves the peer ClientHello ALPN offer, selects one configured local protocol deterministically, and emits that selection inside the existing local EncryptedExtensions continuation without widening the landed crypto floor.
risks: If the server selects a configured protocol without checking the peer offer, the interop lane can look green while violating authenticated application-protocol negotiation.

If missing or unsupported ALPN is discovered only after local ServerHello or Handshake keys have already been published, the server overclaims handshake progress on a branch that should fail with no_application_protocol.

If listener or server-auth configuration does not reach the managed key schedule deterministically, the live server path will continue to emit EncryptedExtensions without ALPN even though public configuration already requires one.
design_summary: The managed server-role path keeps ALPN selection behind the existing transcript-progress and key-schedule seam rather than adding a second TLS stack or broad handshake policy layer. The transcript owner continues to validate the ClientHello shape, but the server EncryptedExtensions continuation now also preserves enough of the peer ALPN offer list to let the key schedule select one configured local protocol deterministically by local configuration order. The listener/runtime path threads the already-validated local application-protocol list into the managed TLS bridge before handshake progression begins. During server-role ClientHello processing, the key schedule selects one mutual protocol before local ServerHello publication; if no mutual protocol exists or the peer omits ALPN entirely, the branch fails deterministically with no_application_protocol and does not publish ServerHello, Handshake keys, or later handshake messages. When a match exists, the existing REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113 EncryptedExtensions continuation formats one local ALPN extension carrying only that selected protocol alongside the local QUIC transport parameters on the existing handshake seam.
alternatives_considered: Select the first configured server protocol without preserving the peer offer. Rejected because that would hide a protocol-negotiation mismatch instead of proving an honest overlap.

Add ALPN to the EncryptedExtensions message unconditionally whenever local configuration exists. Rejected because a peer can omit ALPN or offer only unsupported values, which must fail rather than being silently papered over.

Push ALPN selection into the listener shell or public callback surface. Rejected because the negotiated handshake bytes are owned by the managed TLS bridge and EncryptedExtensions formatter, not by the adapter shell.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The local protocol list must already have been validated against the listener and server-auth configuration before it reaches the managed TLS bridge.
Selection is deterministic by local configuration order, not by peer-offer order.
Missing ALPN, duplicate ALPN, malformed ALPN, or no-overlap ALPN fails with no_application_protocol before ServerHello publication.

The slice does not add broader TLS application-policy negotiation, x25519, certificate-flight changes, 0-RTT, endpoint-host wiring beyond passing existing validated configuration inward, or interop-runner classification changes.
